name: Cappuccino Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Hazelnuts (toasted and skinned, chopped coarsely)
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the hazelnuts in a preheated 350°F oven for 10 to 15 minutes until lightly colored and skins blistered. Let cool, then wrap in kitchen towel and steam for 1 minute. Rub nuts in towel to remove as much skin as possible.
2. In an electric mixer with paddle attachment, blend flour, sugar, baking soda, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and cloves until combined well.
3. Whisk espresso, milk, egg yolk, and vanilla; add to flour mixture, beating until a dough forms. Stir in hazelnuts and chocolate chips.
4. Turn out dough onto floured surface and knead several times. Halve the dough.
5. Form each piece into a flattish log (12 inches long by 2 inches wide) on a large buttered, floured baking sheet, spacing logs at least 3 inches apart.
6. Bake logs in middle of preheated 350°F oven for 35 minutes. Let cool on rack for about 10 minutes.
7. Reduce oven temperature to 300°F. Cut logs crosswise into 3/4-inch slices. Arrange biscotti cut sides down on baking sheet; bake 5 to 6 minutes per side until pale golden.
8. Transfer to racks and store in airtight containers. Makes about 32 biscotti.
